spec_match.rationale: Walks the chromestatus motivation literally: each step stamps a key, simulated crash emits the Reporting payload with the breadcrumbs attached.

interactive.rationale: Five-step fake checkout (real buttons that stamp keys), a danger 'crash' button, a live ribbon, a key table and a wire payload.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb says walk a fake checkout then crash; the page is that flow.

edge_cases.rationale: Happy path only — what if breadcrumbs overflow the budget? What if the user back-buttons? What if the crash is in a worker (different Document)?

references.rationale: Includes the correct chromestatus.com/feature/6228675846209536 link in see-also.

design_system.rationale: Variables only, danger button overrides background and color together (--accent-rose / --bg-ivory).

openQuestions.title: Show ring-buffer eviction
openQuestions.detail: Real breadcrumb systems (Sentry, Crashlytics) cap to ~100 events and drop oldest. The KV API has its own budget. Add a long flow (50 steps) and surface what happens at the budget boundary — does the oldest crumb fall off, does the set call reject, does it truncate the value?

openQuestions.title: Worker crash with cross-context breadcrumbs
openQuestions.detail: A renderer crash in a Worker doesn't have access to the main thread's crashReporter. Show a worker concept that posts breadcrumbs via postMessage to the main thread, which stamps them — that's the real shape for workers.

summary: The flagship motivation demo, and it lands well — the checkout walk plus crash button makes the abstract spec concrete. Add budget/eviction behaviour and the worker-bridge angle to make it spec-complete.
